description: GitHub Copilot CLI settings for AxioDB - Embedded NoSQL database for Node.js
instructions: CRITICAL: Always run 'npm run build' after ANY code change - TypeScript errors in production are unacceptable
CRITICAL: Update tests in Test/modules/ for ANY feature change (crud.test.js, transaction.test.js, read.test.js)
CRITICAL: Never leave tasks incomplete - Done means: build passes, tests pass, docs updated, no regressions
Follow SOLID principles - Single responsibility, DRY, no code duplication
TypeScript strict mode - No 'any' types, use proper interfaces, null checks, type guards
Respect singleton pattern - Only one AxioDB instance allowed, tests run in separate processes
Use dual-write pattern for indexes - Write to both memory (speed) and disk (durability)
Random cache TTL 5-15 minutes to prevent cache stampede
Always validate input, sanitize paths to prevent traversal attacks
Use InMemoryCache before disk reads for performance
Prefer Promise.all for batch operations instead of sequential await
Update documentation when changing features: README.md, Document/, Dockerfile, JSDoc

singleton.description: AxioDB uses singleton pattern - only one instance allowed
singleton.implementation: private static _instance: AxioDB; throw error if instance exists

dualWrite.description: Write to memory cache AND disk for indexes

source.Services: Core operations (Database, Collection, CRUD, Index, Transaction, Aggregation)

structure.Test: Test files in separate processes (crud.test.js, transaction.test.js, read.test.js)

security: Validate all input - check types, reject arrays as documents
Use AES-256 encryption for sensitive collections
Never log sensitive data (passwords, encryption keys)
Return generic error messages to users, log detailed errors
performance: Use InMemoryCache with random TTL (5-15 min)
Use Map for O(1) lookups instead of Array.find O(n)
Avoid unnecessary disk I/O - read once, process in memory
antiPatterns: Never duplicate code - extract to helpers (DRY)
Never use sequential operations when parallel is possible
Never use magic strings - use enums or const objects
Never use hacky solutions or temporary fixes
